creator: bbauman@wgate.com
text:
Here's some mail we received from Max Khon regarding this problem. He was kind enough to include a patch, which can be obtained by doing a diff of revisions 1.8 and 1.9 of elf32-i386.c in the CVS tree.

Could someone please apply this patch or upgrade binutils to a more recent version? It makes debugging Mozilla much more pleasant :-).

Thanks.

hi, there!
On Thu, 7 Sep 2000, Bruce Bauman wrote:
> Thanks for your prompt reply. The e-mail I received didn't include an
> attachment. I grabbed a copy of binutils-2.10.0.24 from a mirror site, but
> it contained many changes from the version currently in RELENG_4.
sorry, forgot to attach file (as usual :)
> Is there a place where I can view the changes to the binutils tree via
> cvsweb or something like that? Or is it easier for you to send us a patch?
http://sources.redhat.com/binutils/
you should apply patch for libbfd/elf32-i386.c (1.8 -> 1.9)
I attached a patch anyway.
/fjoe

bfd.diff

--- contrib/binutils/bfd/elf32-i386.c.orig Fri Aug 18 12:17:38 2000
+++ contrib/binutils/bfd/elf32-i386.c Fri Aug 18 12:18:09 2000
@@ -1293,7 +1293,9 @@
     sections against symbols defined externally
     in shared libraries.  We can't do anything
     with them here.  */
-   || (input_section->flags & SEC_DEBUGGING) != 0)))
+   || ((input_section->flags & SEC_DEBUGGING) != 0
+       && (h->elf_link_hash_flags
+   & ELF_LINK_HASH_DEF_DYNAMIC) != 0))))
{
  /* In these cases, we don't need the relocation
                      value.  We check specially because in some
